steps.title: Use the trend to rule out negative slopes
steps.content: The scatterplot's points climb from lower-left to upper-right, so the line of best fit has a positive slope. Choices A, B, and C all have slope $-0.9$, so they cannot fit increasing data. Only choice D, with slope $+0.9$, has the right sign.

steps.title: Sanity-check the fit at two scatter points
steps.content: Approximate endpoints from the plot are $(1, 3)$ and $(9, 10)$, giving slope $\frac{10-3}{9-1} = \frac{7}{8} \approx 0.9$ and intercept $b \approx 3 - 0.9(1) \approx 2.1$. The line $y = 0.9x + 2.2$ matches both the slope and intercept of choice D — D is correct.
